page: Microsoft Azure F80ams_v7 is an x86_64 architecture server belonging to the Famsv7 family. It is equipped with 80 dedicated physical cores and 80 vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor. The system features 640.0 GB of RAM, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. The processor includes an L1d cache of 48 KB, an L2 cache of 1.0 MB, and an L3 cache of 32 MB. It supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 (including AVX512F, AVX512_VNNI, and AVX512_BF16) and cryptography standards like AES and SHA_NI. No local storage or GPUs are bundled with this instance, though it is premium storage capable.

Benchmark results place the F80ams_v7 in the top tier (top 10%) across multiple performance dimensions. It achieves a stress-ng single-core score of 3778.46 and a multi-core score of 288363.19. Memory bandwidth is exceptionally high, with cached small-block reads reaching 14097.63 GB/sec and large-block uncached reads at 476.74 GB/sec. Database operations are highly efficient, as demonstrated by a PassMark database score of 41561.37 and Redis SET throughput of 202.55 million operations per second. For natural language processing, the server processes 7B parameter LLM prompts at 349.11 tokens per second and generates text at 54.05 tokens per second.

Given its high memory density of 8.0 GB per core and dedicated physical CPU allocation, the F80ams_v7 offers strong qualitative cost efficiency for memory-intensive enterprise workloads that do not require local storage. The absence of bundled local storage means storage costs are decoupled, allowing users to scale premium remote storage independently. This server is highly suited for large-scale database operations, high-throughput web serving, in-memory caching, and CPU-based LLM inference tasks.
description: The Microsoft Azure F80ams_v7 is a memory-intensive server from the Famsv7 family, featuring 80 dedicated physical cores powered by an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor on the x86_64 architecture. It is equipped with 640.0 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core, but includes no local storage or GPUs. Operating under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, the instance delivers top-tier performance across benchmarks, including a stress-ng multi-core score of 288363.19 and a Redis SET throughput of 202.55 million operations per second. It also demonstrates strong capabilities in CPU-based LLM inference, processing 7B parameter model prompts at 349.11 tokens per second. This configuration is highly efficient for memory-heavy workloads, large-scale database operations, in-memory caching, and high-throughput web serving where independent premium storage scaling is preferred.
og_description: Microsoft Azure F80ams_v7 is an 80-core AMD EPYC memory-intensive server with 640 GB RAM. It delivers top-tier database, memory bandwidth, and CPU-based LLM inference performance under Microsoft Hyper-V.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure F80ams_v7 is an 80-core AMD EPYC server with 640 GB RAM, optimized for memory-intensive databases and high-throughput workloads.
tagline: An 80-core memory-intensive server featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processors and 640 GB RAM for high-throughput database and memory-heavy workloads.
bullet_points: 80 dedicated AMD EPYC 9V45 physical cores on x86_64 architecture
640.0 GB of RAM providing a high 8.0 GB per core memory density
Top-tier memory bandwidth and database processing performance
Capable of high-speed CPU-based LLM inference and Redis operations
No local storage bundled, allowing decoupled premium storage scaling
Best suited for memory-intensive databases, caching, and web serving
